description_long: This is a compact, timber-framed barn with a steeply pitched gable roof constructed from weathered wooden planks. The exterior walls are finished with a whitewashed, rough-textured infill between exposed wooden beams, giving it a traditional half-timbered appearance. The front facade features a single, centrally placed wooden door beneath a small, rectangular louvered vent or window set high in the gable. The side elevations each have two small, rectangular windows set symmetrically beneath the eaves. The roof is made of wide, overlapping wooden boards, complete with visible iron hinges and latches, suggesting the presence of loft access or ventilation panels. The overall structure is modest in scale, evoking the style of rural European or early colonial agricultural outbuildings.
description: A small, whitewashed timber-framed barn with a steep gable roof, central wooden door, and small windows beneath the eaves. The structure features exposed beams and weathered wood, evoking a traditional rural style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central wooden door, exposed timber framing, and a small louvered vent or window high in the gable.
description_view_1: Side/top view highlighting the steep, weathered wooden gable roof with visible iron hinges and two small windows beneath the eaves.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, with a central door and louvered vent or window in the gable.
description_view_3: Opposite side/top view, mirroring view 1, with the same roof features and window arrangement.
attribute_door_style: single hinged wooden door with simple rectangular design
attribute_window_arrangement: one small louvered window or vent high in the gable on each end, two small rectangular windows on each side beneath the eaves
